methods.description: Procedure for collecting foraminiferal specimens from the water column, preserving them, and preparing them for subsequent analysis through washing, isolation, drying, sieving, selection, and cleaning.

steps.description: Foraminiferal specimens of Globigerina bulloides, Globorotalia inflata, Globigerinita glutinata and Neogloboquadrina incompta were sampled from the water column at standardised depth intervals (0-20 m, 20-40 m, 40-60 m, 40-60 m, 60-80 m, 80-100 m, 100-200 m, 200-300 m, 300-500 m, 500-700 m, 700-1000 m, 1000-1500 m, 1500-2000 m, 2000-2500 m) using a multiple opening-closing net (for additional detail on sampling procedures, refer to Schiebel et al., 1995).
steps.searchable_summary: sample foraminifera from water column at specified depth intervals using a net
inputs: water column at specified depth intervals

steps.description: Formalin (4%) was added to the samples in order to retard remineralisation of organic material, which engenders carbonate dissolution, and buffered using hexamethyltetramine to a pH of 8.2.
steps.searchable_summary: add formalin and hexamethyltetramine to samples for preservation

steps.description: If samples were stored for prolonged periods, regular pH measurements were carried out and additional hexamethyltetramine added if necessary to prevent degradation.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor and adjust sample pH during storage

steps.description: Post-cruise, samples were washed over a 100 µm sieve using tap water.
steps.searchable_summary: wash samples over a 100 µm sieve

steps.description: Specimens were then isolated from the sample using a pipette
steps.searchable_summary: isolate individual foraminifera specimens from sample residue

steps.description: and dried at room temperature in glass petri dishes before sieving.
steps.searchable_summary: dry isolated specimens at room temperature

steps.description: Only specimens of G. bulloides that did not possess spines were employed as part of this study.
steps.searchable_summary: select specific species and morphotypes (G. bulloides without spines)
outputs: selected G. bulloides specimens without spines

steps.description: Specimens were delicately brush-cleaned to remove adhered material not associated with the foraminifera prior to analysis.

methods.description: Procedure for measuring the size (diameter and area) of selected foraminiferal specimens using microscopy and image analysis.

steps.description: A minimum of 10 specimens (mean = 19) from the 200-250 µm fraction were picked
steps.searchable_summary: select specimens from the 200-250 µm size fraction

steps.description: uniformly orientated with umbilical side facing upwards

and measured using an integrated, semi-automated microscope and image analysis system (cf.Bollmann et al., 2004).Images were acquired using Leica Z16 APO apochromatic monocular microscope and integrated 12 megapixel Olympus CC12 colour camera (at 5 times magnification)
steps.searchable_summary: acquire images of oriented specimens using microscope and camera

steps.description: and processed with analySIS software (Olympus, version 5).
steps.searchable_summary: process images using image analysis software

steps.description: The image analysis system provides mean values for the test silhouette area and test diameter for each aliquot, where diameter is taken to be the mean of the diameters which bisect the centre of the foraminiferal test, as observed in 2-D.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean test silhouette area and mean test diameter per aliquot from image analysis results
outputs: mean test silhouette area per aliquot

methods.method_name: Foraminiferal Specimen Weighing and Mass Calculation
methods.description: Procedure for weighing dried foraminiferal specimens to determine Shell Biomass Weight (SBW) and calculating Normalized Biomass Weight (MBW) based on size measurements.

steps.description: Following size-analysis, the tests were placed into preweighed aluminium capsules
steps.searchable_summary: place sized specimens into preweighed aluminium capsules
inputs: sized foraminifera specimens (from size analysis method)

steps.description: and transferred to an environmentally controlled weighing room.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer capsules with specimens to controlled weighing room
outputs: capsules with specimens in weighing room

steps.description: The samples were left for a minimum of 12 h to equilibrate with the ambient atmospheric moisture content before being weighed
steps.searchable_summary: equilibrate samples to ambient atmospheric moisture in weighing room
inputs: capsules with specimens in weighing room

steps.description: using a Satorious ME5 balance (precision = 0.001 mg) to obtain SBW.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh equilibrated samples to obtain Shell Biomass Weight (SBW)

steps.description: MBW was calculated by normalising SBW to the mean diameter (MBW diam ) and area (MBW area ) for the corresponding species.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Normalized Biomass Weight (MBW) by normalizing SBW to mean diameter and mean area
inputs: mean test diameter per aliquot (from size analysis method)
mean test area per aliquot (from size analysis method)
